title: Cereaster extensus
taxonomy: Cereaster extensus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to China and is also known as the Chinese sea star or the starfish flower. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Cereaster extensus is closely related to other daisy-like plants and is classified as a type of daisy. The plant is known for its unique star-shaped flowers and is often grown in gardens for its ornamental value. Cereaster extensus is a perennial plant, meaning it comes back year after year in areas with suitable climates.

intro: Cereaster extensus, also known as the Chinese Cereaster, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in China and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, diabetes, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cereaster extensus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cereaster extensus is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
